verses.1: The king’s heart is in the LORD’s hand like the watercourses.
verses.2: Every way of a man is right in his own eyes,
verses.4: A high look and a proud heart,
verses.5: The plans of the diligent surely lead to profit;
verses.6: Getting treasures by a lying tongue
verses.7: The violence of the wicked will drive them away,
verses.8: The way of the guilty is devious,
verses.9: It is better to dwell in the corner of the housetop
verses.10: The soul of the wicked desires evil;
verses.11: When the mocker is punished, the simple gains wisdom.
verses.12: The Righteous One considers the house of the wicked,
verses.13: Whoever stops his ears at the cry of the poor,
verses.14: A gift in secret pacifies anger,
verses.15: It is joy to the righteous to do justice;
verses.16: The man who wanders out of the way of understanding
verses.17: He who loves pleasure will be a poor man.
verses.18: The wicked is a ransom for the righteous,
verses.19: It is better to dwell in a desert land,
verses.20: There is precious treasure and oil in the dwelling of the wise,
verses.21: He who follows after righteousness and kindness
verses.22: A wise man scales the city of the mighty,
verses.23: Whoever guards his mouth and his tongue
verses.24: The proud and arrogant man—“Scoffer” is his name—
verses.25: The desire of the sluggard kills him,
verses.26: There are those who covet greedily all day long;
verses.27: The sacrifice of the wicked is an abomination—
verses.29: A wicked man hardens his face;
verses.30: There is no wisdom nor understanding
verses.31: The horse is prepared for the day of battle;
